title: prunus × foveata
intro: Prunus × foveata, also known as the Japanese plum or Japanese apricot, is a hybrid tree species derived from the cross between Prunus salicina and Prunus mume. The bark, leaves, and fruit of this plant possess medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where they are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Prunus × foveata include its ability to aid digestion, relieve coughs, and help lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of dried bark, leaves, or fruit, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
